codeReview.summary: The gist file targeting fix in the Rust backend is correct, and the beacon panel now filters using the configured machine identifier. However, the new Copy action in SyncBeaconSettingsSection copies the persisted config gist ID instead of the visible/editable gistIdInput value, so after a user edits the field to join a different beacon, the Copy button can share the wrong Gist ID until validation/save completes.

issues.description: `handleCopyGist()` writes `config.syncBeaconGistId` to the clipboard even though the input is now always editable and driven by `gistIdInput`. If a user pastes a new Gist ID to join another beacon and clicks Copy before blur/validation saves it, the UI copies the old persisted Gist ID rather than the value shown in the field, breaking the sharing/join workflow introduced by this feature. The copy action should use the currently displayed/trimmed input value or be clearly scoped to the saved ID only.

summary: Reviewed commit `2efbc64` plus the worker transcript/handoff for `fix-beacon-gist-robustness-and-ui`. Backend gist file targeting and self-filtering alignment look correct, but the always-visible Gist ID input/copy UX still has a blocking mismatch: the Copy button shares the stale saved ID instead of the editable field value.
